name: Saw Palmetto Extract
summary: Saw palmetto extract has high-quality negative evidence for BPH symptom improvement versus placebo despite plausible anti-androgen mechanisms.
description: Saw palmetto extract has high-quality negative evidence for BPH symptom improvement versus placebo despite plausible anti-androgen mechanisms.
safetyNotes: GI upset, headache; caution anticoagulants and hormone-sensitive conditions.

claimMap.claim: v9.2: Large JAMA RCT found saw palmetto no better than placebo at 320–960 mg/day; block monetized recommendation.
claimMap.notes: v9.2: Large JAMA RCT found saw palmetto no better than placebo at 320–960 mg/day; block monetized recommendation.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: 320 mg/day extract was tested; up to 960 mg/day showed no benefit in a large RCT

safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: v9.2: Large JAMA RCT found saw palmetto no better than placebo at 320–960 mg/day; block monetized recommendation.

contraindications: Pregnancy or breastfeeding without clinician guidance (anti-androgenic hormone activity)
hormone-sensitive conditions such as prostate cancer without clinician review
anticoagulant or antiplatelet therapy or planned surgery (case reports of increased intraoperative and postoperative bleeding)
May interact with hormonal contraceptives or other hormone therapies given anti-androgenic activity
mild GI upset such as nausea or diarrhea is the most common side effect.
